opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: He caught this hard, rough bump on his finger while playing, and it just won't stop bleeding. It's bleeding much more than a normal cut.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: My child snagged a rough bump on his finger and it won't stop bleeding.

symptom_narrative.onset: rough bumps on the fingers for ~6 months; snagged the biggest one an hour ago
symptom_narrative.character: rough, fleshy grey bumps; the torn one is bleeding from a vascular base
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: bleeding won't stop with tissue pressure; child tearful
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: back of the right index finger (and the middle finger)
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: rough surface, interrupted skin lines, tiny black dots at the base; several similar bumps (from scratching); no fever
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: the bumps have been there for months

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: multiple rough finger bumps for ~6 months

objective_data_offered.value: a ~6mm rough fleshy-grey bump on the back of the index finger with the top torn off and a vascular base oozing blood; the skin lines are interrupted and there are tiny black dots; several similar bumps on the next finger; no surrounding redness/pus
oozing from the torn base, more than a normal cut, slows with firm pressure
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: warts are vascular and bleed readily
